facts.text: Levittown was officially named on January 1, 1948, marking the birth of America’s first mass‑produced suburban community.
The town grew from four distinct developments: Green Acres, Levitt, Omega, and Miller, together housing over 18,000 homes by the early 1950s.
Levittown’s original houses were built on concrete slabs using pre‑cut lumber shipped from Levitt & Sons’ own California factories.
The Little Red Schoolhouse, built in 1876, still stands as a reminder of the area’s pre‑suburban roots.
Billy Joel, the famed singer‑songwriter, grew up in a Levitt‑built house in nearby Hicksville, a testament to the town’s influence on American music.
In 1985, a historic landmark was designated on Old Jerusalem Road, just across from MacArthur High School, preserving a piece of Levittown’s early history.
